title: Component and End-to-End RAG Evaluation · Contract Workshop
summary: A retrieval engineer and domain partner incrementally build layered RAG evaluation harness, change one query, document, tenant, language, model, index, or policy condition, and keep earlier Python, provenance, accessibility, security, evaluation, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of layered RAG evaluation harness while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Evaluate ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citations, safety, system quality, and user outcome separately and together.
Measure candidate recall, ranking, context precision and recall, source diversity, conflict coverage, and citation-map integrity.
Measure answer relevance, claim support, faithfulness, citation correctness, completeness, conflict handling, abstention, safety, and usefulness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rag Component E2e Separation
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop RAG engineering team handles case 9481 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to evaluate retrieval, grounding, answer utility, citations, safety, and human evidence separately; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rag Component E2e Separation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for evaluate ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citations, safety, system quality, and user outcome separately and together. For RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rag Component E2e Separation, keep Rag Component E2e Separation connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

Build deterministic Python evidence to evaluate retrieval, grounding, answer utility, citations, safety, and human evidence separately; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall. steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Rag Human Outcome Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-human-outcome-evidence-kwcj54" that uses case 4011 to evaluate retrieval, grounding, answer utility, citations, safety, and human evidence separately. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Rag Human Outcome Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Human Outcome Evidence into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Rag Human Outcome Evidence, keep Rag Human Outcome Evidence connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-human-outcome-evidence-kwcj54
# Competency: rag-human-outcome-evidence.
# Case 4011: an enterprise retrieval migration canary.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data.
def evidence_rag_human_outcome_evidence_kwcj54(retrieval_score: float = 0.82, faithfulness: float = 0.91, answer_relevance: float = 0.84, citation_precision: float = 0.95, safety_pass: bool = True, human_reviewed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_kwcj54 = "kwcj54-45jcwk-4011"
    assert evidence_variant_kwcj54
    evidence_axes_kwcj54 = (
        "corpuskwcj54",
        "query45jcwk",
        "tenant4011",
        "indexkwcj544011",
        "prompt45jcwk4011",
        "evaluation4011kwcj54",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_kwcj54)) == 6
    scores = (retrieval_score, faithfulness, answer_relevance, citation_precision)
    if any(score < 0.0 or score > 1.0 for score in scores):
        return False, 0.0, "metric-range"
    if min(scores) < 0.75:
        return False, min(scores), "weak-stage"
    if not safety_pass:
        return False, min(scores), "safety-failure"
    if not human_reviewed:
        return False, min(scores), "judge-only"
    aggregate = sum(scores) / len(scores)
    assert aggregate <= 1.0
    return True, round(aggregate, 4), "layered-evaluation"
